# Obsidian Volleyball Academy — Complete Information

> Premium junior volleyball academy in Baulkham Hills, Sydney Hills District, Australia. Holiday camps and term programs for juniors aged 8–18. Volleyball NSW affiliated. Active Kids Voucher accepted.

## Business Details

- Business Name: Obsidian Volleyball Academy (OVA)
- Type: B2C junior volleyball academy
- Founded: 2025
- Owner: Dan
- Email: obsidianvolleyball@gmail.com
- Instagram: @obsidianvolleyball
- Website: obsidianvolleyball.com
- Booking URL: obsidianvolleyball.as.me
- Affiliation: Volleyball NSW (volleyballnsw.org.au)
- Venue: Baulkham Hills High School, Windsor Road, Baulkham Hills NSW 2153
- Region: Hills District, Western Sydney, New South Wales, Australia

## Programs Offered

### Holiday Camps
- When: School holiday periods — April, July, October, January
- Duration: Full-day (9AM–1PM) and half-day sessions available
- Pricing: $200 for 5-day camp package (includes free OVA shirt). Individual day and half-day rates also available.
- Skill Levels: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced
- Ages: 8–18

### Weekly Term Programs
- When: During school terms
- Frequency: Weekly sessions
- Skill Levels: All levels

### Beginner Blast (Ages 8–12)
For players with zero experience. Foundational skills in a supportive environment:
- Rules and court positions
- Serving, passing, setting basics
- Small group coaching with high coach-to-player ratio
- Confidence building through structured play

### Skill Builder (Ages 10–16)
For intermediate players preparing for competition:
- Advanced serving and attacking skills
- Defence and blocking fundamentals
- Tactical game play and positioning
- Preparation for club and school competition

### Elite Program (Ages 13–18)
High-intensity performance volleyball for serious competitors:
- Coaching aligned with Volleyball NSW pathways
- Video analysis and tactical debriefs
- Physical conditioning components
- Pathway to representative and club volleyball

## Coaching Team

### Melinda — Head Coach & Co-Founder
- Volleyball NSW Accredited Coach — Level 1
- First Aid Certified (HLTAID011)
- Working With Children Check (NSW)
- Junior Development Program Facilitator
- Specialises in beginner and intermediate development
- Founded OVA in 2025 after competitive volleyball career

### Jessica — Lead Coach
- Volleyball NSW Accredited Coach — Level 1
- First Aid Certified (HLTAID011)
- Working With Children Check (NSW)
- Competition and Club Coaching Experience
- Leads intermediate and advanced sessions
- Known for breaking complex skills into learnable steps

## Coaching Philosophy
1. Athlete-First: Every decision made with athlete development and enjoyment in mind
2. Technical Excellence: No shortcuts on technique; correct fundamentals early
3. Safe & Inclusive: All coaches WWCC and First Aid certified; welcoming to all
4. Pathway Focused: Aligned with Volleyball NSW for club and representative progression

## Location Details

### Venue
Baulkham Hills High School
Windsor Road, Baulkham Hills NSW 2153
Hills District, Sydney

### Facilities
- Multiple indoor courts
- All-weather (indoor facility)
- Free on-site parking

### Nearby Suburbs Served
Baulkham Hills, Castle Hill, Kellyville, Cherrybrook, Bella Vista, Winston Hills, Northmead, North Rocks, Carlingford, West Pennant Hills, Dural, Norwest, Rouse Hill, The Ponds, Stanhope Gardens

## Payment & Vouchers

- Payment: Credit/debit cards via Stripe
- Active Kids Voucher: Accepted — NSW Government voucher worth up to $100 per child per year
- Booking: Online via Acuity Scheduling at obsidianvolleyball.as.me

## What to Bring
1. Sports shoes (clean, non-marking soles)
2. Large water bottle
3. Lunch and snacks for full-day camps
4. Hat and sunscreen for outdoor warm-ups
5. Active Kids Voucher code if applicable

## Comprehensive FAQ

Q: Do you accept Active Kids Vouchers?
A: Yes. NSW Government Active Kids Vouchers accepted, worth up to $100 per child per year. Enter voucher code at checkout or bring along to the session.

Q: What age groups do you cater for?
A: Juniors aged 8 to 18. Players grouped by skill level, not just age.

Q: Where are the camps held?
A: Baulkham Hills High School in the Hills District of Sydney. Multiple indoor courts — sessions run rain or shine.

Q: My child has never played volleyball — is that OK?
A: Absolutely. The Beginner Blast program is designed for kids with zero experience. Coaches make it fun and non-intimidating.

Q: How do I book?
A: Book online at obsidianvolleyball.as.me. Select your program and time slot, pay securely, and receive a confirmation email.

Q: How much does it cost?
A: 5-day holiday camp package is $200 (includes free OVA shirt). Individual day and half-day rates also available. Check booking page for current pricing.

Q: Can I book a single day?
A: Yes. Both full camp packages and individual day bookings are available.

Q: Do you run programs during school terms?
A: Yes. Weekly term programs run during the school year in addition to holiday camps.

Q: Are coaches qualified?
A: All coaches hold Volleyball NSW accreditation, Working With Children Checks (WWCC), and First Aid certifications (HLTAID011).

Q: What is the coach-to-player ratio?
A: Small groups maintained to ensure meaningful coaching attention. Ratios vary by program level.

Q: Is there parking?
A: Free on-site parking available. Enter via school main entrance, follow signs to sports hall.

Q: What time do sessions start?
A: Full-day camps run 9AM to 1PM. Half-day options available. Check booking page for specific times.

Q: Is there a pathway to competitive volleyball?
A: Yes. OVA aligns with Volleyball NSW pathways. The Elite Program prepares advanced juniors for club and representative volleyball.

Q: What is your refund policy?
A: Contact obsidianvolleyball@gmail.com for refund enquiries. Handled case-by-case.

## Competitor Context (Hills District Volleyball)
Competitors in the area include Baulkham Hills Volleyball (BHV), ProVolley, Just Spike It, and Sydney Thunder. OVA differentiates through premium coaching quality, Volleyball NSW affiliation, skill-based grouping, and a dedicated Hills District venue.
